input.background: Claim amounts for wind damage to insured homes are independent random variables with a common probability density function $$f(x)= \begin{cases}3 x^{-4} & \text { for } x \geq 1 \\ 0 & \text { otherwise }\end{cases}$$ where $x$ is the amount of a claim in thousands. Suppose 3 such claims will be made.
input.question: What is the expected value of the smallest of the three claims?

output.answer: The expected value of the smallest of the three claims is $1125$.
